_comment: STATE POSTURE LAYER — Kimley-Horn Nuclear Task Force. Hand-maintained friendliness/activity layer, separate from the auto-updated statutory restriction layer in regulatory_data.json. Per state: score 0-4 (0 hostile statutory barrier, 1 restrictive/geographic, 2 neutral standard pathway, 3 favorable/active, 4 leading), signals (concrete 2025-26 actions), entity (state nuclear office/consortium/task force), fleet (has operating reactors), engage (Task Force engagement angle). Sourced from NEI/ANS 'Where States Stand on Nuclear' (Jan 2026), NCSL, DOE, and state legislation. The map combines this with the statutory tier to display a Hostile->Leading favorability read.

scale.0: Hostile — statewide moratorium / high statutory barrier
scale.2: Neutral — no statewide restriction; standard NRC + state PUC pathway
scale.3: Favorable — active state support (studies, clean-energy classification, infrastructure)
scale.4: Leading — incentives, dedicated office, and/or directed deployment

signals: Operating fleet (Browns Ferry, Farley) — workforce + supply chain
AL.engage: Strong existing-fleet workforce and TVA momentum; site/permitting support for SMR siting near load.

AK.engage: Remote/defense microreactor niche; Army regulatory pathway at Eielson.

signals: Gov. Hobbs EO — Energy Promise Task Force (advanced nuclear focus)
APS/SRP/TEP partnership evaluating new nuclear sites
AZ.engage: Active utility site-evaluation consortium (APS/SRP/TEP) — early civil/siting partner opportunity. Palo Verde workforce base.

AR.engage: Feasibility-study phase — positioning for siting/transmission scoping.

signals: 50-yr statutory moratorium pending federal HLW solution
CA.engage: Statutory barrier; near-term work limited to existing-fleet support and agency education.

signals: Expanded clean-energy definition to include nuclear
CO.engage: Clean-energy reclassification opens procurement; strong coal-retirement (energy-community) siting candidates.

signals: Moratorium repealed — municipal opt-in for advanced reactors (Act 25-173)
Advanced Nuclear Reactor Site Readiness Funding Program
CT.engage: Municipal opt-in model — community engagement + site readiness funding are direct Task Force entry points.

signals: Established feasibility-focused task force / working group

FL.engage: State assessment complete; utility-led uprate and new-build scoping.

signals: Only new large reactors built in a generation (Vogtle 3 & 4)
Demonstrated AP1000 construction workforce + supply chain
GA.engage: Proven new-build state; Vogtle workforce and supply chain are national assets for follow-on projects.

HI.engage: Constitutional barrier; exploratory working group only. Island microgrid context.

ID.entity: Idaho Advanced Nuclear Energy Task Force
signals: Gov. Little EO — Advanced Nuclear Energy Task Force
ID.engage: Most active siting framework in the West; INL host + state RFI on site-selection factors — prime civil/siting engagement.

signals: Repealed 300 MW reactor size cap (Jan 2026) — open to all classes
IL.engage: Newly uncapped; large merchant fleet + data-center demand. Confirm downstate (MISO) vs ComEd (PJM) market split for siting.

IN.engage: Among the most aggressive incentive stacks; cost recovery + manufacturing credit. AEP SMR interest. Manufacturing + siting both in play.

signals: Duane Arnold restart (Google PPA, FERC approved)
IA.engage: Restart pathway active; existing site infrastructure and transmission.

KS.engage: No statewide restriction; emerging microreactor activity. SPP-served.

signals: PSC directed to develop a nuclear siting process + public hearings
KY.engage: PSC actively building a siting process — direct opportunity to shape siting/permitting criteria. Strong coal-community (energy-community) base.

LA.engage: Study phase; industrial Gulf Coast load + existing fleet workforce.

signals: Statewide voter-approval requirement + federal waste-pathway condition
ME.engage: High statutory barrier (referendum). Education/agency-coordination only near-term.

signals: PSC working group on nuclear procurement program

MA.entity: MA Roadmap for Advanced Nuclear and Fusion
signals: Gov. Healey UMass-Lowell advanced nuclear & fusion roadmap
MA.engage: Statutory barrier but strong executive roadmap and repeal momentum — watch closely; positioning state.

signals: Holtec Pioneer SMR site (CP Part 1 filed)
MI.engage: First U.S. restart + co-located SMR new-build at Palisades — flagship reference site.

MN.engage: Outright ban; repeal pending. Existing fleet (Monticello, Prairie Island). Watch SF350-type repeal.

MS.engage: No statewide restriction; existing-fleet workforce. MISO South.

MO.engage: No statewide restriction. Split across MISO/SPP — confirm market for siting.

signals: NPPD statewide siting study — narrowing 16 communities to 3-4 sites
NE.engage: Active utility-led site down-selection — time-sensitive siting/civil engagement window.

NV.engage: Data-center demand (Las Vegas); no state program yet. CAISO/WECC context.

NH.engage: Clean-energy reclassification; ISO-NE. NGA New England convening participant.

signals: Bill for state-backed 1.2 GW incentive program
NJ.engage: Just opened + state task force + incentive bill — high-momentum new market. Holtec Oyster Creek SMR interest.

NM.engage: No state program identified; uranium-supply context. SPP (eastern) territory.

signals: Geographic moratorium (Long Island / former LILCO area only)
Gov. Hochul directed NYPA to build >=1 GW advanced nuclear
NY.engage: No statewide bar — strong executive push (NYPA 1 GW). Long Island counties restricted (see county detail). Upstate favorable.

signals: CWIP expansion — utilities collect financing costs during construction
Duke Belews Creek SMR (6 designs evaluated)
NC.engage: Construction-financing reform materially de-risks new build; Duke actively evaluating SMR site.

ND.engage: Study phase; coal-transition (energy-community) siting candidates. MISO/SPP split.

OH.engage: No statewide restriction; strong PJM load growth + existing-fleet workforce.

signals: Ban pending federal HLW repository + statewide vote
OR.engage: High statutory barrier (repository + referendum). Education only near-term.

signals: Crane Clean Energy Center (TMI-1) restart — Microsoft 20-yr PPA
PA.engage: Flagship restart + behind-the-meter data-center PPAs; deep fleet workforce. Top-tier market.

signals: Now allows utilities to procure/contract nuclear power (exception)
RI.engage: Statutory barrier softening — procurement now allowed. ISO-NE.

signals: Large operating fleet (Catawba, Oconee, Summer, Robinson)
SC.engage: Idled AP1000 site (V.C. Summer) + deep fleet workforce — strong new-build candidate.

SD.engage: No state program identified. MISO/SPP split.

signals: $50M to TVA to accelerate SMR construction + $10M workforce
TVA Clinch River BWRX-300; Kairos Hermes 1 & 2
TN.engage: Densest advanced-reactor cluster (Clinch River, Kairos) + state funding + TVA. National hub.

signals: Texas Advanced Nuclear Energy Office — single permitting point of contact
Multiple pilots (Natura, Last Energy, X-energy Dow); Fermi AP1000s
TX.engage: Single-point-of-contact permitting office is a major siting accelerator. Confirm ERCOT vs SPP/MISO split for any site. Top-tier market.

UT.entity: Utah nuclear energy consortium / state energy council
signals: Nuclear energy consortium + state energy council
UT.engage: Operation Gigawatt + Brigham City hub + multistate compact — aggressive deployment posture. Creekstone 2 GW data-center MOU.

VT.engage: Statutory barrier; former Vermont Yankee site. Education/agency-coordination near-term.

signals: $122M cost recovery for Appalachian Power SMR site evaluation
VNEC 2025-2029 strategic plan + Virginia Innovative Nuclear Hub
Dominion North Anna SMR; AEP Joshua Falls ESP
Amazon-Dominion MOU; data-center capital of the world
VA.engage: Priority market: SMR cost recovery + data-center demand + Dominion/AEP activity. Confirm SB 454 / HB 1491 status when scoping.

WA.engage: Amazon-backed Xe-100 cluster + Hanford workforce; strong siting base around existing nuclear reservation.

WV.engage: Recently opened + studying; among the strongest coal-transition (energy-community) siting bases in the country.

signals: First state to repeal its moratorium (2016)
TerraPower Natrium — NRC construction permit issued (Mar 2026)
BWXT BANR microreactor; Valar Atomics pilot
WY.engage: Hosts the furthest-along advanced reactor new build (Natrium, Kemmerer) on a coal-transition site — reference energy-community siting case.
